fields.label: Reference Code
fields.description: Content-channel key — = the linked ERPNext Purchase Order name. Stable from PO submit; ERPNext-born. The §9 push uses this as `referenceCode` on CreatePurchaseOrder so EE can dedupe + match content updates.

fields.label: Status
fields.description: §9 review/exception state — same enum as §8 master maps. Mapped = pushed and clean; Created-Flagged = pushed with a soft issue (e.g. unmapped tax rule); Flagged-Not-Created = not pushed (Supplier Map missing, Item Map missing, Gate-0 non-EE warehouse); Drift = po_status divergence between ERPNext-pushed and EE-observed; Disabled = lifecycle-deactivated.

fields.label: Purchase Order
fields.description: The ERPNext PO this row tracks. Dynamic-link to Purchase Order; the §9 push uses doc.name as reference_code (autoname formula ties the two together). One PO Map row per in-scope PO (Gate-0: warehouse must map to an EE Location).

fields.label: EasyEcom Internal IDs
fields.description: §9 two-channel two-key model: reference_code (above) is the WRITE key on the content channel (CreatePurchaseOrder), ee_po_id is the WRITE key on the status channel (updatePoStatus). EE returns po_id from CreatePurchaseOrder data — captured then for all subsequent status pushes.

fields.label: EE po_id (status-channel key)
fields.description: EasyEcom-assigned int returned by /WMS/Cart/CreatePurchaseOrder as data.poId. Status channel key on /wms/updatePoStatus. NULL until the first successful content push returns it. Re-pushes of content (createOrUpdate=U) do NOT reassign this — the int is sticky once captured.

fields.label: PO Status Tracking
fields.description: Drift surface for the status channel. PO content is submitted-immutable on the ERPNext side, so there is no content-snapshot field on this row — the only drift surface is po_status. Status drift is detected by comparing last_pushed_po_status (what ERPNext last asserted) against ee_observed_po_status (what GRN-pull most recently observed). Divergence indicating EE-side action contrary to ERPNext (e.g. EE→4 Rejected when ERPNext shows Approved=3) → Drift + Integration Discrepancy in §9 Stage 3.

fields.label: Last Pushed po_status
fields.description: po_status int last sent via /wms/updatePoStatus. Idempotency guard for the §9 Stage 2 status pusher — skip the push if (target po_status) == last_pushed_po_status. NULL until the first status push. ERPNext-side status transitions: on_submit → 3 (Approved), receipts-complete → 5 (Completed), on_cancel → 7 (Cancelled).

fields.label: EE Observed po_status
fields.description: po_status_id last observed via /Grn/V2/getGrnDetails (§9 Stage 3 sweep). Refreshed every time a GRN row carries this PO. The reconciliation compares against last_pushed_po_status; divergence indicating EE-side action contrary to ERPNext raises a Discrepancy. EE-internal codes (11-16 = shipping milestones) are recorded but NOT raised as Discrepancy.

fields.label: Observed At
fields.description: When ee_observed_po_status was last refreshed by the §9 Stage 3 GRN-pull sweep.

fields.label: Flag Reason
fields.description: For Created-Flagged / Flagged-Not-Created / Drift rows: plain-English reason the FDE can act on (e.g. 'Supplier Map missing for supplier VEND-001', 'Gate-0 fail: warehouse not EE-mapped', 'tax_rule not configured on Item ITEM-X', 'po_status divergence: pushed 3, EE shows 7').

fields.label: Drift Details
fields.description: Populated by the §9 Stage 3 status reconciliation. Drift on a PO Map row is status-only by design — PO documents are submitted-immutable in ERPNext (content drift is structurally impossible). The Drift Fields table here holds at most one row: the po_status divergence.

fields.label: Drift Fields
fields.description: Per-field divergence. Reuses §8d/§8e/§8f's shared Drift Field child DocType — the §9 status drift case stores field='po_status', erpnext_value=last_pushed_po_status, ee_value=ee_observed_po_status.

fields.label: Drift Exclusions
fields.description: Fields the drift detector should IGNORE on this PO. Rarely populated for §9 (only po_status is even drift-comparable here). Reuses the shared Exclude Field child.
